channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Federal workers confront mass confusion as Musk's deadline to list accomplishments looms
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Confusion and chaos loom as hundreds of thousands of federal employees begin their workweek on Monday facing a deadline from President Donald Trump’s cost-cutting chief, Elon Musk, to explain their recent accomplishments or risk losing their jobs.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, ERIC TUCKER and AMANDA SEITZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Key federal agencies refuse to comply with Musk's latest demand in his cost-cutting crusade
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Key U.S. agencies, including the FBI, State Department and the Pentagon, have instructed their employees not to comply with cost-cutting chief Elon Musk's latest demand that federal workers explain what they accomplished last week — or risk losing their job.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, ERIC TUCKER and AMANDA SEITZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: The Trump administration is firing 2,000 USAID workers and putting thousands of others on leave
item.description: The Trump administration said Sunday that it was placing all but a fraction of staffers at the U.S. Agency for International Development on leave worldwide and eliminating 2,000 U.S.-based staff positions.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 2,000 USAID staff to be cut, others put on administrative leave
item.description: “There is no one left to do any work,” the USAID official said, requesting anonymity to protect against retaliation.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Nearly 200 Venezuelan migrants are flown home from Guantanamo Bay, with a layover in Honduras
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Nearly 200 Venezuelan immigrants to the U.S. were returned to their home country after being detained at Guantanamo Bay, in a flurry of flights that forged an unprecedented pathway for U.S. deportations. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE and REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: How possible USPS changes could affect your mail
item.description: “We want to have a post office that works well and doesn’t lose massive amounts of money," Trump said. “We’re thinking about doing that. And it’ll be a form of a merger, but it’ll remain the Postal Service, and I think it’ll operate a lot better.
item.dc:creator: Brad Hamilton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hochul says she made a case to Trump for NYC's congestion pricing, yet predicts courts will decide
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York Gov. Kathy Hochul says she told President Donald Trump in a private meeting at the White House that congestion pricing tolls in New York City are necessary and working, yet the Democrat predicted the courts will likely decide the matter.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Motive in Pennsylvania hospital shooting unclear, but officials say man had recent contact with ICU
item.description: Investigators are trying to determine why a man targeted a Pennsylvania hospital, taking workers inside the intensive care unit hostage and holding one staff member at gunpoint as her hands were bound by zip ties.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: One dead, one injured after shooting at New Mexico Air Force base
item.description: Kirtland Air Force base said the 377th Security Forces Squadron was called to an incident at approximately 2 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Beware of text messages about unpaid tolls, FBI and state authorities warn
item.description: Drivers should be on the lookout for scam text messages warning of an unpaid road toll bill, according to recent warnings from authorities.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Lynne Marie Stewart of 'Pee-wee's Playhouse,' 'It's Always Sunny' dies: reports
item.description: The actress has recently finished filming for another season of "It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia" and her forthcoming pickleball-related film, "Dink.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Uber feature may overcharge you. Here's how to turn it off
item.description: Unless you disable it, a new setting in the app may end up costing you the next time you hail a ride overseas.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hawaii man freed after 30 years in prison visits mother's grave and ponders ubiquitous cellphones
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — One of the first places Gordon Cordeiro visited when a judge ordered him released after spending 30 years in prison for a killing he always maintained he had nothing to do with was his mother’s Hawaii gravesite.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: California governor asks Congress for nearly $40 billion for Los Angeles wildfire relief
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom has asked Congress to approve nearly $40 billion in aid to help the Los Angeles area recover from January's devastating wildfires, which he said could become the costliest natural disaster in U.S. history.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Can you spot a credit card skimmer? What to look for
item.description: Before putting your card into a reader, the FBI says there are some easy and effective ways to protect your information.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Food recalls increase, more deadly in 2024: Report
item.description: Hospitalizations and deaths caused by contaminated foods doubled in 2024, according to a new Public Interest Research Group report.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Roblox is 'hunting ground for child sex predators,' new lawsuit claims
item.description: The lawsuit was filed earlier this month on behalf of a 13-year-old boy who was an "avid user of Roblox and Discord.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, Fox 5

item.title: Universal reveals Shrek, SpongeBob lands for new theme park in Texas
item.description: Universal Destinations & Experiences recently revealed several themed areas that will be featured at Universal Kids Resort, a new family-focused theme park set to open next year in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, Fox 5

item.title: Video shows barefoot foster child with no coat left in snow, begging to go back inside
item.description: A man in Haskell, Oklahoma, heard a child nearby begging to go back inside. When he saw the little girl standing outside in record-breaking freezing temperatures and wearing only pajamas, he called 911.
item.dc:creator: Heather Holeman/KFOR, NewsNation

item.title: Delta faces first lawsuits days after plane flips in Toronto
item.description: All 21 people injured in the crash landing have been released from the hospital, and no one was killed.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Luigi Mangione draws crowd for first court hearing since arraignment in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The defendant wore a bulletproof vest and shackles. A woman in the crowd wore a “Free Luigi" scarf. Outside, throngs of people cheered and chanted his name.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Luigi Mangione makes first court appearance since arraignment in UnitedHealthcare CEO's death
item.description: Luigi Mangione, 26, has pleaded not guilty to multiple counts of murder, including murder as an act of terrorism, in the Dec. 4 killing of Brian Thompson outside a midtown Manhattan hotel.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, KRON4

item.title: One-armed basketball player makes history with basket
item.description: When her high school basketball coach cut her from the team senior year, Baileigh Sinaman-Daniel refused to let that rejection, or the fact she was born with one arm, end her dream.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Forever 21 may close remaining stores as second bankruptcy looms
item.description: Fashion retailer Forever 21 has begun liquidation sales at several of its locations throughout the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, ABC 4

item.title: Speaker Johnson discourages DOGE stimulus checks idea
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) threw cold water on the idea of using savings from the Trump administration’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) government-slashing activities to send checks to Americans.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, ABC 4

item.title: Connor McDavid scores in OT to give Canada 3-2 win over United States in 4 Nations Face-Off final
item.description: Instead, the Canadian team stood at the blue line, arm in arm, player and coach, wearing their championship hats while the maple leaf flag was lowered behind the 4 Nations Face-Off trophy and the national anthem reverberated across the Americans' home ice.
item.dc:creator: JIMMY GOLEN AP Sports Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How LA wildfires are making an already tough rental market even worse
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The one-bedroom cottage with a woodsy vibe reminded Heather McAlpine of the home she lost to the brutal Los Angeles-area wildfires. But only two hours after seeing the listing, the rental was snapped up.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR and DAMIAN DOVARGANES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Latest: Judge declines to halt Trump administration's federal workers layoffs
item.description: A federal judge on Thursday refused to temporarily block the Trump administration’s mass layoff of federal workers while a lawsuit brought by five unions moves forward. U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former Colorado sheriff’s deputy convicted of homicide in shooting death of man in crisis
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A former Colorado sheriff’s deputy was convicted of criminally negligent homicide Thursday in the shooting death of a 22-year-old man in distress who called 911 for help after his car got stuck in a small mountain community.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Get a text about a $1,400 IRS stimulus check? Don't click the link, BBB says
item.description: If you get a text message claiming that you will receive a $1,400 check from the IRS, don’t click any links. The Better Business Bureau warns that it’s a scam.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, WGN 9
